title: Smoky Crispy British Cauliflower Gratin
description: A creamy, herb-infused cauliflower gratin with a smoky paprika kick, finished crisp under the broiler for a satisfying weeknight British comfort dish.
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F). Grease a 2-quart baking dish with a little butter or non-stick spray.
In a large skillet, heat 1 tablespoon olive oil over medium heat. Add chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 4 minutes.
Add minced garlic, dried thyme, rosemary, smoked paprika, and liquid smoke. Cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Toss in cauliflower florets, season with salt and pepper, and stir to coat with aromatics. Cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Meanwhile, make the cheese sauce: in a small saucepan, heat milk over medium until just steaming. Whisk in grated cheddar cheese, stirring until melted and smooth. Remove from heat.
Stir in Greek yogurt and xanthan gum until fully incorporated and thickened. Taste and adjust seasoning if needed.
Pour the cheese sauce over the cauliflower in the baking dish, spreading evenly. Sprinkle additional grated cheddar on top if desired.
Bake in the preheated oven for 20 minutes, or until the top is golden and bubbly.
Broil for 2-3 minutes for a crispy finish, watching closely to avoid burning.
Remove from oven, let rest 5 minutes, then garnish with chopped fresh parsley or additional herbs before serving.
